text_fr: Sachant les  autres  initiatives  importantes  qu'elle  a  prises  pour  mieux  faire prendre  conscience  de  la  souffrance  des  victimes  du  racisme,  de  la  discrimination raciale,  de  la  xÃĐnophobie  et  de  l'intolÃĐrance  qui  y  est  associÃĐe  et  de  toutes  les formes  de  discrimination,  notamment  d'un  point  de  vue  historique,  en  particulier celles  qui concernent la commÃĐmoration des victimes de l'esclavage et de la traite transatlantique des esclaves,

text_fr: Rappelant le  Statut  du  Tribunal  de  Nuremberg  et  le  jugement  rendu  par  ce tribunal, qui a reconnu comme criminelles, notamment, l'organisation SS et chacune de  ses  composantes,  dont  la  Waffen-SS,  du  fait  que  ses  membres  officiels  ont  ÃĐtÃĐ impliquÃĐs  dans  la  commission  de  crimes  de  guerre  et  de  crimes  contre  l'humanitÃĐ dans  le  contexte  de  la  Seconde  Guerre  mondiale  ou  en  ont  eu  connaissance,  ainsi que les autres dispositions pertinentes du Statut et du jugement,
text_en: Recalling the Statute of the Nuremberg Tribunal and the judgement of the Nuremberg Tribunal, which recognized as criminal, inter alia, the SS organization and each of its components, including the Waffen-SS, the fact that its official members were involved in, or were aware of, the commission of war crimes and crimes against humanity in the context of the Second World War, as well as other relevant provisions of the Statute and the judgement,

text_en: Alarmed at the proliferation in many parts of the world of various extremist political parties, movements and groups, including neo-Nazis and skinheads, as well as racist and extremist movements and ideologies, and the fact that this phenomenon has resulted in the establishment of discriminatory measures, policies and laws at the local and national levels,

text_en: Deeply concerned at all recent acts of violence and terrorism caused by violent nationalism, racism, xenophobia and related intolerance, including at sporting events,

text_en: Noting with deep concern the alarming increase in the number of cases of discrimination, intolerance and extremist violence motivated by anti-Semitism, Islamophobia and Christianophobia and prejudice against persons of different ethnic origin, religion or belief,

text_en: Bearing in mind the atrocities of the Second World War, and stressing in this regard that it was, inter alia, the victory over Nazism that led to the creation of the United Nations, which was called upon to prevent further wars and to protect future generations from that scourge,

items_en: 2. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Special Rapporteur of the Human Rights Council on contemporary forms of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance prepared in response to the request contained in General Assembly resolution 71/179 11;

3. Expresses its appreciation to the High Commissioner and the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights for their efforts to combat racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, including the holding by the Office of the High Commissioner of the database on practical ways to combat racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;

4. Expresses deep concern at the glorification, in any form, of the Nazi movement, neo-Nazism and former members of the Waffen-SS organization, in particular through the construction of monuments and commemorative works and the organization of public demonstrations to the glory of the Nazi past, the Nazi movement and neo-Nazism, as well as the declaration or ingenuity to declare that these members and those who fought against the anti-Hitler coalition and collaborated with the Nazi movement participated in national liberation movements;

5. Calls for universal ratification and effective implementation of the International Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination, 3 and encourages States parties that have not yet done so to consider making the declaration provided for in article 14 of the Convention, thereby recognizing the competence of the Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination to receive and consider communications from individuals or groups of persons within their jurisdiction claiming to be victims of a violation by a State party of any of the rights set forth in the Convention;
6. Encourages States to adopt the necessary legislation to combat racism while ensuring that the definition of racial discrimination contained therein is consistent with article 1 of the Convention;
7. Encourages States that have made reservations to article 4 of the Convention to give serious and priority consideration to withdrawing them, as emphasized by the Special Rapporteur;

8. Recalls that any legislative or constitutional measures adopted to combat extremist parties, movements and groups, including neo-Nazis and skinheads, and similar extremist ideological movements, must comply with relevant international human rights standards, in particular articles 4 and 5 of the Convention and articles 19 to 22 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights;
9. Encourages States parties to the Convention to ensure that their legislation incorporates the provisions of the Convention, including those of article 4;

10. Re-emphasizes the Special Rapporteur ' s recommendation that "States should prohibit any official or non-official commemorative ceremony of the Nazi regime, its allies and related organizations" 12 , and stresses that such events insult the memory of the countless victims of the Second World War and have a negative impact on children

text_en: and youth, in this regard, the importance of States taking measures, in accordance with international human rights law, to combat any event organized to the glory of the SS organization and any of its components, including the Waffen-SS, and the failure of States that do not effectively combat such practices to comply with the obligations of States Members of the United Nations under the Charter of the United Nations;

14. RÃĐaffirme que  ces  actes  peuvent  ÃŠtre  considÃĐrÃĐs  comme  relevant  du champ d'application de la Convention, que l'on ne saurait les justifier lorsqu'ils ne relÃĻvent pas du droit Ã  la libertÃĐ de rÃĐunion pacifique et d'association ni du droit Ã  la libertÃĐ d'expression, et qu'ils peuvent relever de l'article 20 du Pacte international relatif aux droits civils et politiques et faire l'objet de restrictions en application des articles 19, 21 et 22 du Pacte ;

Encourage les  Ãtats  Ã   prendre  des  mesures  concrÃĻtes,  notamment lÃĐgislatives  et  ÃĐducatives,  pour  faire  obstacle  au  rÃĐvisionnisme  concernant  la Seconde  Guerre  mondiale  et  Ã   la  nÃĐgation  des  crimes  contre  l'humanitÃĐ  et  des crimes de guerre commis durant la Seconde Guerre mondiale ;

Condamne sans  rÃĐserve tout  dÃĐni  ou  tentative  de  dÃĐni  de  l'Holocauste, ainsi  que  toute  manifestation  d'intolÃĐrance  religieuse,  d'incitation  Ã   la  haine,  de harcÃĻlement ou de violence Ã  l'ÃĐgard de personnes ou de communautÃĐs en raison de leur appartenance ethnique ou de leurs croyances religieuses ;

17. Se  fÃĐlicite que  le  Rapporteur  spÃĐcial  ait  demandÃĐ  que  soient  prÃĐservÃĐs activement les  sites  oÃđ,  pendant  l'Holocauste,  les  nazis  avaient  installÃĐ  des  camps de la mort, des camps de concentration, des camps de travail forcÃĐ ou des prisons, et ait  engagÃĐ  les  Ãtats  Ã   prendre  des  mesures,  notamment  lÃĐgislatives,  rÃĐpressives  et ÃĐducatives, pour mettre fin Ã  toutes les formes de dÃĐni de l'Holocauste 14 ;

Engage les  Ãtats  Ã   continuer  de  prendre,  dans  le  respect  du  droit international des droits de l'homme, des mesures adÃĐquates, notamment lÃĐgislatives, afin de prÃĐvenir les incitations Ã  la haine et Ã  la violence Ã  l'encontre de personnes en  situation  de  vulnÃĐrabilitÃĐ  et,  le  cas  ÃĐchÃĐant,  Ã   envisager  de  rÃĐexaminer  leur
items_en: 11. Expresses its deep concern at the increased frequency of attempts and acts of desecration or demolition of monuments erected in memory of those who fought Nazism during the Second World War, as well as of unlawful exhumation or removal of the remains of such persons, and in this regard urges States to fulfil fully their obligations, including in accordance with article 34 of Additional Protocol I to the Geneva Conventions of 1949; 13

12. Strongly condemns acts of glorification and promotion of Nazism, such as the execution of graffiti and pronazi paintings, especially on monuments dedicated to the memory of the victims of the Second World War;

13. Notes with concern the increase in racist acts throughout the world, in particular the rise of skinhead groups, which are responsible for many of these acts, as well as the resurgence of racist and xenophobic violence, such as arson at homes and ransacks of schools and places of worship, targeting, inter alia, persons belonging to national, ethnic, religious or linguistic minorities, or committed for any other reason;

14. Reaffirms that such acts may be regarded as falling within the scope of the Convention and cannot be justified when they do not fall within the scope of the right to freedom of peaceful assembly and association or the right to freedom of expression, and that they may fall within the scope of article 20 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights and be subject to restrictions under articles 19, 21 and 22 of the Covenant;

15. Encourages States to take concrete measures, including legislative and educational measures, to prevent the revisionism of the Second World War and the denial of crimes against humanity and war crimes committed during the Second World War;

16. Condemns without reservation any denial or attempt to deny the Holocaust, as well as any manifestation of religious intolerance, incitement to hatred, harassment or violence against persons or communities on account of their ethnicity or religious beliefs;

17. Welcomes the request of the Special Rapporteur for the active preservation of sites where during the Holocaust the Nazis had established death camps, concentration camps, forced labour camps or prisons, and calls upon States to take measures, including legislative, repressive and educational measures, to end all forms of Holocaust denial; 14

18. Calls upon States, in accordance with international human rights law, to continue to take adequate measures, including legislative measures, to prevent incitement to hatred and violence against persons in vulnerable situations and, where appropriate, to consider reviewing their

text_fr: lÃĐgislation de lutte contre le racisme compte tenu du fait que les discours de haine et les incitations Ã  la violence se font de plus en plus ostensibles contre ces personnes  ;
text_en: anti-racism legislation in view of the growing ostensibility of hate speech and incitement to violence against such persons;

items_fr: 19. Se  dÃĐclare  profondÃĐment  prÃĐoccupÃĐe face  aux  tentatives  d'exploitation commerciale  par  la  publicitÃĐ  des  souffrances  des  victimes  des  crimes  de  guerre  et des  crimes  contre  l'humanitÃĐ  commis  pendant  la  Seconde  Guerre  mondiale  par  le rÃĐgime nazi ;

20. Souligne que  les  pratiques  susmentionnÃĐes  font  injure  Ã   la  mÃĐmoire des innombrables  victimes  des  crimes  contre  l'humanitÃĐ  commis  durant  la  Seconde Guerre mondiale, en particulier ceux commis par l'organisation SS et par ceux qui ont luttÃĐ contre la coalition antihitlÃĐrienne et collaborÃĐ avec le mouvement nazi, et peuvent avoir une influence nÃĐfaste sur les enfants et les jeunes, et que les Ãtats qui ne  combattent  pas  effectivement  ces  pratiques  manquent  aux  obligations  que  la Charte des Nations Unies impose aux Ãtats Membres de l'Organisation des Nations Unies, notamment celles liÃĐes aux buts et aux principes de l'Organisation ;

21. Souligne  ÃĐgalement que  toutes  ces  pratiques  alimentent  les  formes contemporaines  de  racisme,  de  discrimination  raciale,  de  xÃĐnophobie  et  de l'intolÃĐrance qui y est associÃĐe et contribuent Ã  la propagation et Ã  la multiplication de  divers  partis  politiques,  mouvements  et  groupes  extrÃĐmistes,  notamment  de nÃĐonazis et de skinheads, et appelle Ã  cet ÃĐgard Ã  une vigilance accrue ;

22. Constate  avec  inquiÃĐtude que  les  dangers  que  les  partis  politiques, mouvements  et  groupes  extrÃĐmistes  reprÃĐsentent  pour  les  droits  de  l'homme  et  la dÃĐmocratie sont universels et qu'aucun pays n'en est Ã  l'abri ;

23. Insiste sur la nÃĐcessitÃĐ de prendre les mesures qui s'imposent pour mettre fin aux pratiques susvisÃĐes et engage les Ãtats et toutes les autres parties prenantes Ã  adopter des mesures plus efficaces, dans le respect du droit international des droits de  l'homme,  pour  prÃĐvenir  et  combattre  ces  phÃĐnomÃĻnes  et  les  mouvements extrÃĐmistes,  qui  font  peser  une  rÃĐelle  menace  sur  les  valeurs  dÃĐmocratiques,  Ã  redoubler de vigilance et Ã  se  montrer ÃĐnergiques en intensifiant leurs efforts pour cerner ces dÃĐfis et les relever vÃĐritablement ;

24. Souligne l'importance  des  donnÃĐes  et  statistiques  sur  les  infractions racistes et xÃĐnophobes pour ce qui est de recenser les types d'infractions commises et le profil de leurs victimes et auteurs et de dÃĐterminer si ces derniers sont affiliÃĐs Ã  des  mouvements  ou  groupes  extrÃĐmistes,  ce  qui  permet  de  mieux  comprendre  ces phÃĐnomÃĻnes  et  de  dÃĐfinir  des  mesures  pour  lutter  efficacement  contre  de  telles infractions,  et  rappelle  Ã   cet  ÃĐgard  les  engagements  pris  dans  le  Programme  de dÃĐveloppement durable Ã  l'horizon 2030 15 en ce qui concerne les donnÃĐes, le suivi et l'application  du  principe  de  responsabilitÃĐ,  y  compris  la  collecte  de  donnÃĐes ventilÃĐes ;

25. Encourage les  Ãtats  Ã   prendre  de  nouvelles  dispositions  en  vue  de dispenser  aux  services  de  police  et  aux  autres  forces  de  maintien  de  l'ordre  une formation  sur  les  idÃĐologies  des  partis  politiques,  mouvements  et  groupes extrÃĐmistes  dont  la  propagande  constitue  une  incitation  Ã   la  violence  raciste  et xÃĐnophobe,  Ã   renforcer  leur  capacitÃĐ  de  lutter  contre  les  infractions  racistes  et xÃĐnophobes, Ã  s'acquitter de la responsabilitÃĐ qui leur incombe de traduire en justice les auteurs de telles infractions et Ã  lutter contre l'impunitÃĐ ;

26. Constate avec une vive inquiÃĐtude que  le  nombre  de  siÃĻges occupÃĐs par des  reprÃĐsentants  de  partis  extrÃĐmistes  Ã   caractÃĻre  raciste  ou  xÃĐnophobe  dans plusieurs parlements locaux et nationaux a augmentÃĐ et souligne, Ã  cet ÃĐgard, qu'il
items_en: 19. Expresses its deep concern at attempts to exploit commercially through publicity of the suffering of the victims of war crimes and crimes against humanity committed during the Second World War by the Nazi regime;

20. Stresses that the above-mentioned practices insult the memory of the countless victims of crimes against humanity committed during the Second World War, in particular those committed by the SS organization and those who fought against the anti-Hitler coalition and collaborated with the Nazi movement, and may have a negative impact on children and young people, and that States that do not effectively combat such practices are in breach of the obligations imposed by the Charter of the United Nations on States Members of the United Nations, including those related to the purposes and principles of the Organization;

21. Also emphasizes that all such practices fuel contemporary forms of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance and contribute to the spread and proliferation of various extremist political parties, movements and groups, including neo-Nazis and skinheads, and in this regard calls for increased vigilance;
22. Expresses concern that the dangers to human rights and democracy posed by extremist political parties, movements and groups are universal and that no country is immune to them;

23. Stresses the need to take the necessary measures to put an end to the above-mentioned practices, and calls upon States and all other stakeholders to adopt more effective measures, in accordance with international human rights law, to prevent and combat these phenomena and extremist movements, which pose a real threat to democratic values, to intensify vigilance and to be energetic by intensifying their efforts to identify and respond to these challenges;

24. Stresses the importance of data and statistics on racist and xenophobic crimes in identifying the types of crimes committed and the profiles of their victims and perpetrators and in determining whether they are affiliated with extremist movements or groups, thereby enhancing understanding of these phenomena and identifying measures to effectively combat such crimes, and recalls in this regard the commitments made in the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda 15 with regard to data, monitoring and accountability, including the collection of disaggregated data;

25. Encourages States to take further steps to train police and other law enforcement agencies on the ideologies of extremist political parties, movements and groups whose propaganda constitutes incitement to racist and xenophobic violence, to strengthen their capacity to combat racist and xenophobic offences, to fulfil their responsibility to bring to justice those responsible for such offences and to combat impunity;

26. Expresses deep concern that the number of seats held by representatives of racist or xenophobic extremist parties in several local and national parliaments has increased, and stresses, in this regard, that

text_fr: faut que tous les partis politiques dÃĐmocratiques fondent leurs programmes et leurs activitÃĐs  sur  le  respect  des  droits  de  l'homme  et  des  libertÃĐs  fondamentales,  la dÃĐmocratie,  l'ÃĐtat  de  droit  et  la  bonne  gouvernance,  et  qu'ils  condamnent  tous  les messages diffusant des idÃĐes fondÃĐes sur la supÃĐrioritÃĐ ou la haine raciales et ayant pour  but  d'alimenter  les  formes  contemporaines  de  racisme,  de  discrimination raciale, de xÃĐnophobie et de l'intolÃĐrance qui y est associÃĐe ;
text_en: All democratic political parties must base their programmes and activities on respect for human rights and fundamental freedoms, democracy, the rule of law and good governance, and condemn all messages that disseminate ideas based on racial superiority or hatred and that aim to fuel contemporary forms of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;

items_fr: 27. Se dit profondÃĐment prÃĐoccupÃĐe par la multiplication des actes racistes et xÃĐnophobes  signalÃĐs  lors  de  manifestations  sportives,  commis  notamment  par  des groupes extrÃĐmistes, dont des groupes de nÃĐonazis et de skinheads, et demande aux Ãtats,  aux  fÃĐdÃĐrations  sportives  et  aux  autres  parties  prenantes  concernÃĐes  de renforcer  les  mesures  visant  Ã   prÃĐvenir  de  tels  actes,  tout  en  saluant  les  mesures prises  par  de  nombreux  Ãtats  et  fÃĐdÃĐrations  ou  clubs  sportifs  pour  ÃĐliminer  le racisme  des  manifestations  sportives,  notamment  par  des  activitÃĐs  sportives pratiquÃĐes sans aucune discrimination et dans l'esprit olympique, qui reposent sur la comprÃĐhension entre les ÃŠtres humains, la tolÃĐrance, l'intÃĐgration, le franc-jeu et la solidaritÃĐ ;

30. Rappelle la recommandation du Rapporteur spÃĐcial qui invite les Ãtats Ã  incorporer dans leur droit pÃĐnal une disposition prÃĐvoyant que les motivations ou les objectifs racistes ou xÃĐnophobes d'une infraction sont des circonstances aggravantes qui autorisent des peines plus lourdes 17 , et encourage les Ãtats dont la lÃĐgislation ne comporte pas une telle disposition Ã  tenir compte de cette recommandation ;

31. Prend  note des mesures  prises  par  les  Ãtats  pour  prÃĐvenir  la discrimination  visant  en  particulier,  mais  non  exclusivement,  les  personnes appartenant  Ã   des  minoritÃĐs  nationales,  ethniques,  religieuses  ou  linguistiques,  les personnes  d'ascendance  africaine,  les  Roms,  les  migrants,  les  rÃĐfugiÃĐs  et  les demandeurs  d'asile,  et  assurer  leur  intÃĐgration  dans  la  sociÃĐtÃĐ,  exhorte  les  Ãtats  Ã  veiller Ã  la mise en Åuvre intÃĐgrale et effective des mesures juridiques, politiques et institutionnelles  visant  Ã   protÃĐger  ces  personnes  et  ces  groupes,  et  recommande qu'ils garantissent effectivement, sans aucune discrimination, leurs droits fondamentaux, notamment le droit Ã  la sÃŧretÃĐ et Ã  la sÃĐcuritÃĐ, Ã  l'accÃĻs Ã  la justice, Ã  une rÃĐparation adÃĐquate et Ã  des informations appropriÃĐes concernant leurs droits, la poursuite  et  la  punition  des  auteurs  d'infractions  racistes  et  xÃĐnophobes  Ã   leur encontre,  ainsi  que  le  droit  de  demander  rÃĐparation  ou  satisfaction  pour  les dommages subis du fait de ces infractions ;

32. Souligne que les racines de l'extrÃĐmisme sont multiples et qu'il faut s'y attaquer en adoptant des mesures adÃĐquates comme l'ÃĐducation, la sensibilisation et la promotion du dialogue et, Ã  cet ÃĐgard, recommande le renforcement des mesures
items_en: 27. Notes with satisfaction, in this regard, that the Special Rapporteur has urged leaders and political parties to strongly condemn any incitement to racial discrimination or xenophobia, to promote tolerance and respect and to refrain from forming coalitions with extremist parties of a racist or xenophobic nature; 16

28. Notes with concern that ethnic profiling and acts of police violence directed against vulnerable persons lead to mistrust of the justice system among victims, which discourages them from seeking redress, and in this regard calls upon States to increase diversity in the police and to adequately punish officials convicted of racist violence or hate speech;

29. Expresses deep concern at the increasing number of racist and xenophobic acts reported at sporting events, including by extremist groups, including neo-Nazi and skinhead groups, and calls upon States, sports federations and other relevant stakeholders to strengthen measures to prevent such acts, while welcoming the measures taken by many States and sports federations or clubs to eliminate racism from sporting events, including through sport activities without discrimination and in the Olympic spirit, based on understanding among human beings, tolerance, integration, free play and solidarity;

30. Recalls the recommendation of the Special Rapporteur that States include in their criminal law a provision that racist or xenophobic motivations or objectives of an offence are aggravating circumstances that permit heavier penalties, and encourages States whose legislation does not include such a provision to take into account this recommendation;

31. Takes note of the measures taken by States to prevent discrimination, in particular, but not exclusively, against persons belonging to national, ethnic, religious or linguistic minorities, persons of African descent, Roma, migrants, refugees and asylum-seekers, and to ensure their integration into society, urges States to ensure the full and effective implementation of legal, political and institutional measures to protect such persons and groups, and recommends that they effectively guarantee, without any discrimination, their fundamental rights, including the right to safety and security, access to justice, adequate reparation and appropriate information regarding their rights, the prosecution and punishment of perpetrators of racist and xenophobic offences against them, and the right to seek redress or satisfaction for the damage suffered as a result of such offences;

32. Stresses that the roots of extremism are multiple and must be addressed through appropriate measures such as education, awareness-raising and the promotion of dialogue, and in this regard recommends the strengthening of measures

text_fr: visant Ã  sensibiliser les jeunes aux dangers des idÃĐologies et des activitÃĐs des partis politiques, mouvements et groupes extrÃĐmistes ;
text_en: to raise awareness among young people of the dangers of ideologies and activities of extremist political parties, movements and groups;

items_fr: 33. 38. RÃĐaffirme l'article 4  de  la  Convention,  aux  termes  duquel  les  Ãtats parties  Ã   cet  instrument  condamnent  toute  propagande  et  toutes  organisations  qui s'inspirent  d'idÃĐes  ou  de  thÃĐories  fondÃĐes  sur  la  supÃĐrioritÃĐ  d'une  race  ou  d'un groupe de personnes d'une certaine couleur ou d'une certaine origine ethnique, ou qui  prÃĐtendent  justifier  ou  encourager  toute  forme  de  haine  et  de  discrimination raciales,  s'engagent  Ã   adopter  immÃĐdiatement  des  mesures  positives  destinÃĐes  Ã  ÃĐliminer toute incitation Ã  une telle discrimination, ou tous actes de discrimination, et,  Ã   cette  fin,  tenant  dÃŧment  compte  des  principes  formulÃĐs  dans  la  DÃĐclaration universelle des droits de l'homme 1 et  des  droits  expressÃĐment ÃĐnoncÃĐs Ã  l'article 5 de la Convention, s'engagent notamment :

a) Ã dÃĐclarer dÃĐlits punissables par la loi toute diffusion d'idÃĐes fondÃĐes sur la  supÃĐrioritÃĐ ou la  haine raciales,  toute incitation  Ã  la  discrimination raciale,  ainsi que tous actes de violence, ou provocation Ã  de tels actes, dirigÃĐs contre toute race ou tout groupe de personnes d'une autre couleur ou d'une autre origine ethnique, de mÃŠme  que  toute  assistance  apportÃĐe  Ã   des  activitÃĐs  racistes,  y compris  leur financement ;

items_en: 33. Reaffirms in this regard that, in order to complement legislative measures, all forms of education, including human rights education, are of particular importance, and invites States, as recommended by the Special Rapporteur, to continue to invest in, inter alia, education, both formal and non-formal, in order to change attitudes and combat ideas of racial hierarchy and superiority and to counter their negative influence, as well as to promote the values of non-discrimination, equality and respect for all;

34. Recognizes the crucial role of education in combating racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, including in promoting the principles of tolerance, integration and respect for ethnic, religious and cultural diversity and in preventing the spread of racist and xenophobic extremist movements and their ideas;

35. Stresses the recommendation made by the Special Rapporteur at its sixty-fourth session, in which he stressed the importance of historical courses in explaining the dramatic events and human suffering that have resulted from the adoption of ideologies such as Nazism and fascism; 18

36. Stresses the importance of other positive measures and initiatives aimed at bringing communities closer together and providing them with a genuine forum for dialogue, such as round tables, working groups and seminars, including training seminars for public officials and media professionals, as well as awareness-raising activities, in particular those conducted by representatives of civil society, to which continued public support is required;

37. Stresses the positive role that relevant United Nations agencies and programmes, in particular the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, can play in the above-mentioned areas;

38. Reaffirms article 4 of the Convention, in which States parties to the Convention condemn all propaganda and organizations based on ideas or theories based on the superiority of a race or group of persons of a certain colour or ethnic origin, or which purport to justify or encourage any form of racial hatred and discrimination, undertake to adopt immediately positive measures to eliminate any incitement to such discrimination, or any acts of discrimination, and, to this end, taking due account of the principles formulated in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights 1 and the rights expressly set forth in article 5 of the Convention, inter alia:

(a) To declare as offences punishable by law any dissemination of ideas based on racial superiority or hatred, any incitement to racial discrimination and any acts of violence, or incitement to such acts, against any race or group of persons of another colour or ethnic origin, as well as any assistance to racist activities, including their financing;

(b) To declare illegal and prohibit organizations, organized propaganda and any other propaganda activities that incite and encourage racial discrimination and to declare participation in such organizations or activities punishable by law;

39. ConsidÃĻre ÃĐgalement que les mÃĐdias peuvent jouer un rÃīle positif dans la lutte contre le racisme, la discrimination raciale, la xÃĐnophobie et l'intolÃĐrance qui y est  associÃĐe,  la  promotion  d'une  culture  de  tolÃĐrance  et  d'intÃĐgration  et  la reprÃĐsentation de la diversitÃĐ d'une sociÃĐtÃĐ multiculturelle ;

45. Encourage les  institutions  nationales de dÃĐfense  des droits de  l'homme, lorsqu'elles  existent,  Ã   ÃĐtablir  des  programmes  visant  Ã   promouvoir  la  tolÃĐrance, l'intÃĐgration et le respect de tous et Ã  recueillir des donnÃĐes Ã  ce sujet ;

46. Note qu'il  importe  de  renforcer  la  coopÃĐration  aux  niveaux  rÃĐgional  et international  en  vue  de  lutter  contre  toutes  les  manifestations  de  racisme,  de discrimination  raciale,  de  xÃĐnophobie  et  de  l'intolÃĐrance  qui  y  est  associÃĐe,  en particulier concernant les questions soulevÃĐes dans la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution ;

47. Souligne qu'il  importe  de  coopÃĐrer  ÃĐtroitement  avec  la  sociÃĐtÃĐ  civile  et les mÃĐcanismes internationaux et rÃĐgionaux de dÃĐfense des droits de l'homme pour lutter  efficacement  contre  toutes  les  manifestations  de  racisme,  de  discrimination raciale,  de  xÃĐnophobie  et  de  l'intolÃĐrance  qui  y  est  associÃĐe  ainsi  que  contre  les partis politiques, mouvements et groupes extrÃĐmistes, notamment de nÃĐonazis et de skinheads, et les mouvements idÃĐologiques extrÃĐmistes de mÃŠme nature qui incitent
items_en: (c) Not to allow public authorities or public institutions, national or local, to incite or encourage racial discrimination;

39. Reaffirms that, as emphasized in paragraph 13 of the outcome document of the Durban Review Conference, any advocacy of national, racial or religious hatred inciting discrimination, hostility or violence should be prohibited by law, that any propagation of ideas based on racial superiority or racial hatred, as well as incitement to racial discrimination and acts of violence or incitement to commit such acts, should be criminalized by law, in accordance with the international obligations of States, and that such prohibitions are compatible with freedom of opinion and expression;

40. Recognizes the positive role that the exercise of the right to freedom of opinion and expression and full respect for the right to freedom to seek, receive and impart information, including through the Internet, can play in the fight against racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;

41. Expresses concern at the increasing use of the Internet to promote and propagate racism, racial hatred, xenophobia, racial discrimination and related intolerance, and in this regard calls upon States parties to the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights to combat the spread of the above-mentioned ideas while respecting their obligations under articles 19 and 20 of the Covenant, which recognize the right to freedom of expression and indicate the grounds on which the exercise of this right may legitimately be restricted;
42. Recognizes the need to promote the use of new information and communications technologies, including the Internet, to combat racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;

43. Also recognizes that the media can play a positive role in combating racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, promoting a culture of tolerance and integration and representing the diversity of a multicultural society;

44. Encourages States, civil society and other stakeholders to make every effort, including through the Internet and social media, to combat, in accordance with international human rights law, the spread of ideas based on racial superiority or racial hatred and to promote the values of equality, non-discrimination, diversity and democracy;
45. Encourages national human rights institutions, where they exist, to establish programmes to promote tolerance, integration and respect for all and to collect data thereon;

46. Notes the importance of strengthening cooperation at the regional and international levels in combating all manifestations of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, in particular with regard to the issues raised in the present resolution;

47. Stresses the importance of close cooperation with civil society and international and regional human rights mechanisms in order to effectively combat all manifestations of racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, as well as extremist political parties, movements and groups, including neo-Nazis and skinheads, and similar extremist ideological movements that encourage

text_en: racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;

items_fr: 48. Rappelle que,  dans  sa  rÃĐsolution  2005/5 5 ,  la  Commission  des  droits  de l'homme a priÃĐ le Rapporteur spÃĐcial de poursuivre sa rÃĐflexion sur la question et de faire les recommandations appropriÃĐes dans ses futurs rapports, en sollicitant et en prenant  en  considÃĐration  les  vues  des  gouvernements  et  des  organisations  non gouvernementales Ã  cet ÃĐgard ;

49. Invite les  Ãtats  Ã   envisager  de  faire  figurer  dans  les  rapports  qu'ils soumettent  pour  l'examen  pÃĐriodique  universel  et  dans  leurs  rapports  aux  organes conventionnels  compÃĐtents  des  informations  sur  les  mesures  prises  pour  lutter contre le racisme, la discrimination raciale, la xÃĐnophobie et l'intolÃĐrance qui y est associÃĐe,  notamment  dans  le  but  de  donner  effet  aux  dispositions  de  la  prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution ;

50. Prie le  Rapporteur  spÃĐcial  d'ÃĐtablir,  en  vue  de  les  lui  prÃĐsenter  Ã   sa soixante-treiziÃĻme session et de les soumettre au Conseil des droits de l'homme Ã  sa trente-huitiÃĻme  session,  des  rapports  sur  l'application  de  la  prÃĐsente  rÃĐsolution,  et l'engage Ã  prÃŠter une attention particuliÃĻre aux paragraphes 4, 9, 10, 11, 13, 18, 19, 34  et  35,  en  se  fondant  sur  les  vues  recueillies  Ã   la  demande  de  la  Commission rappelÃĐe au paragraphe 48 ci-dessus ;

51. Exprime  sa  gratitude aux  gouvernements  et  aux  organisations  non gouvernementales qui ont communiquÃĐ des informations au Rapporteur spÃĐcial lors de l'ÃĐtablissement des rapports qu'il lui a soumis ;

52. Souligne que  ces  informations  sont  importantes  pour  l'ÃĐchange  de donnÃĐes d'expÃĐrience et de pratiques optimales aux fins de la lutte contre les partis politiques,  mouvements  et  groupes  extrÃĐmistes,  notamment  de  nÃĐonazis  et  de skinheads,  et  les  autres  mouvements  idÃĐologiques  extrÃĐmistes  qui  incitent  au racisme,  Ã   la  discrimination  raciale,  Ã   la  xÃĐnophobie  et  Ã   l'intolÃĐrance  qui  y  est associÃĐe ;

53. Encourage les gouvernements et les organisations non gouvernementales Ã   coopÃĐrer  sans  rÃĐserve  avec  le  Rapporteur  spÃĐcial  dans  l'accomplissement  des tÃĒches visÃĐes au paragraphe 50 ci-dessus ;

54. Encourage les  gouvernements,  les  organisations  non  gouvernementales et les  acteurs concernÃĐs Ã  diffuser le plus largement possible, notamment mais non exclusivement par l'intermÃĐdiaire des mÃĐdias, des informations concernant la teneur de la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution et les principes qui y sont ÃĐnoncÃĐs ;
55. DÃĐcide de rester saisie de la question.
items_en: 48. Recalls that, in its resolution 2005/5 5 , the Commission on Human Rights requested the Special Rapporteur to continue his reflection on the issue and to make appropriate recommendations in his future reports, seeking and taking into account the views of Governments and non-governmental organizations in this regard;

49. Invites States to consider including in their reports for the universal periodic review and in their reports to the relevant treaty bodies information on measures taken to combat racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance, including with a view to implementing the provisions of the present resolution;

50. Requests the Special Rapporteur to prepare reports on the implementation of the present resolution for submission to the General Assembly at its seventy-third session and to submit them to the Human Rights Council at its thirty-eighth session, and encourages the Special Rapporteur to pay particular attention to paragraphs 4, 9, 10, 11, 13, 18, 19, 34 and 35, based on the views received at the request of the Commission referred to in paragraph 48 above;

51. Expresses its appreciation to those Governments and non-governmental organizations that have transmitted information to the Special Rapporteur during the preparation of his reports to the General Assembly;

52. Stresses that such information is important for the exchange of experiences and best practices in combating extremist political parties, movements and groups, including neo-Nazis and skinheads, and other extremist ideological movements that incite racism, racial discrimination, xenophobia and related intolerance;
53. Encourages Governments and non-governmental organizations to cooperate fully with the Special Rapporteur in carrying out the tasks referred to in paragraph 50 above;

54. Encourages Governments, non-governmental organizations and relevant actors to disseminate as widely as possible, including but not exclusively through the media, information on the content of the present resolution and the principles contained therein;
55. Decides to remain seized of the matter.
